title: Human-in-the-loop approval gate for irreversible agent actions (git push, deploy, rm -rf)
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) — LLM06 Excessive Agency; codebase gap: hooks.py guardrail blocks per-command but has no approval tier, delegate.py:2569 processes output without gates
description: The guardrail PreToolUse hook can block dangerous Bash but there is no tiered approval — destructive, outward-facing, or irreversible actions (git push, vercel deploy, rm -rf, DROP TABLE) either hard-block or pass silently. OWASP LLM06 recommends requiring explicit approval for high-consequence actions rather than fully autonomous execution. This adds a middle tier: pause and require a human/orchestrator token instead of silently allowing or killing the agent.
proposed_changes: In hooks.py's guardrail (the PreToolUse handler ~181-262), classify commands into allow / require-approval / deny tiers. Add a DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS set in security_utils.py (git push, force-push, deploy, rm -rf, curl|bash, DROP/TRUNCATE, credential writes). For require-approval hits, return exit code 2 with a structured message unless an env-gated token CTO_APPROVED_ACTIONS contains the action hash, or the ticket JSON carries an explicit `approved_actions` list set by the orchestrator/human. Emit a roro event (roro_events.py) 'approval_required' so the Rick Terminal can surface it for one-click approval.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
